_note: SID-43 retrofit: ten paraphrases of the Seed-1 anchor symptom, each preserving the same underlying problem (Maya in data-team-ml can't open the Q3 Revenue Models folder grant-attached to data-team). The threshold (0.21) was calibrated to ONE exact phrasing (the anchor below). This manifest's mutations measure how the threshold holds against realistic surface variation operators might actually type.
_measurement_only: RQ3 option A locked: no pass/fail bar. The retrofit succeeds by honestly producing the score distribution and the false-escalate rate at the current threshold. The structural fix (HyDE query transformation) is chunk-7's job; this measurement is the baseline against which chunk-7 will be measured.
anchor_symptom: Maya on the data team can't open the 'Q3 Revenue Models' folder in Drive. She's in the data-team group, which I checked has access. Can you look?
threshold_derivation: Midpoint of in-domain (0.3875) and off-domain ('what's the weather' → 0.0236) measured against voyage-4-lite on 2026-06-12 (CHUNK2-DESIGN-DECISIONS Q13).

mutations.axis: 1 — pure symptom, casual + minor typos (Slack/chat style)
mutations.rationale: Lowercase, missing punctuation, contractions ('cant', 'u'). Tests whether the threshold survives casual ops-channel typing, which is overwhelmingly common for admin tickets.
Full punctuation, proper capitalization, hedged-formal tone. Tests whether vocabulary stays close enough to the runbook when phrasing is more buttoned-up.
User name stripped ('A data-team member'). Realistic for ticket systems that anonymize before routing. Tests sensitivity to named-entity removal.
Telegraphic fragments, minimal grammar. Tests the threshold against the lowest-information realistic input — sometimes ticket subjects look like this even when the body has more context.

Uses 'permissions' instead of 'access' — a step closer to the runbook's vocabulary without crossing into mechanism language ('inheritance', 'subgroup'). Tests whether partial vocabulary alignment moves the score.

Long, narrative, includes operator's reasoning ('we've already double-checked'). Tests whether additional context dilutes or boosts the score — verbose inputs add unrelated tokens that could either help or hurt cosine similarity.
'Ticket from Maya's manager' framing. Realistic for tiered support workflows. Tests whether report-shaped wrapping affects the score.

mutations.axis: 8 — different verb framing ('blocked' instead of 'can't open')
mutations.rationale: Word-choice variation on the core verb. Tests sensitivity to surface-level verb substitution on the same semantic action.
'I can't get into...' — the end user filing their own ticket. Tests whether the threshold handles the difference between operator-reported and user-self-reported framings.

Almost no shared vocabulary with the anchor: 'analyst' instead of Maya, 'quarterly finance folder' instead of 'Q3 Revenue Models', 'team group' instead of 'data-team', 'view the file' instead of 'open the folder'. Tests the threshold's worst realistic case — semantically identical, lexically distant. Likely the lowest score in the set.
